title: Flat <mml:math xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="inline"><mml:mi mathvariant="normal">Γ</mml:mi></mml:math> Moiré Bands in Twisted Bilayer <mml:math xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="inline"><mml:mrow><mml:msub><mml:mrow><mml:mi>WSe</mml:mi></mml:mrow><mml:mrow><mml:mn>2</mml:mn></mml:mrow></mml:msub></mml:mrow></mml:math>
type1_summary: The archive contains multiple small, structured, figure-specific tabular files that are directly plottable and best interpreted as cleaned source data / replot-ready numerical outputs.
type2_summary: No clear raw/unprocessed instrument-output file is present among the inspected files; the archive appears to contain processed tabular data plus scripts rather than raw detector files.
classification_notes: The inspected contents are dominated by figure-specific tabular numerical data and scripts. Although the paper itself clearly contains raw experimental measurements, none of the inspected files are identifiable as raw instrument output. The archive likely packages processed/source data for figures and model calculations. Rule-based prior suggests hidden Type 2 evidence; review recommended.

file_classifications.file_evidence: This is a 4.2 MB ZIP archive containing extracted tabular data and code-like files. The archive is a container, not directly a figure-ready dataset or a raw instrument file.
file_classifications.reasoning: The archive is best treated as a packaging/container file rather than a dataset type. Its contents include both figure-like tabular data and scripts, so the archive itself is not classifiable as Type 1 or Type 2.

paper_analysis.summary: This paper studies 57.4° twisted bilayer WSe2 using micro-ARPES and micro-LEED, combined with continuum-model analysis, to directly observe a flat moiré miniband derived from monolayer Γ states. The authors find that this Γ-derived flat band lies close in energy to the global valence-band maximum at K, quantify the moiré potential (V0 ≈ 48 meV), and argue that the system is in a strongly correlated regime with possible relevance to the observed correlated phases in twisted WSe2.

figures.description: Schematic of the micro-ARPES experiment on twisted bilayer WSe2 on hBN, including the moiré unit cell concept and sample stack geometry.
AFM topographic image of the van der Waals heterostructure device.
micro-LEED diffraction pattern showing the main diffraction spots and satellite peaks used to determine the moiré reciprocal lattice vector and twist angle.
ARPES constant-energy map 200 meV below the valence-band maximum, showing monolayer Brillouin zones and moiré mini-Brillouin zones.
Energy distribution curves at Γ and K comparing twisted bilayer WSe2 with untwisted 2H WSe2; the twisted sample is fit with multiple Lorentzians convolved with a Gaussian broadening.
ARPES band dispersion of twisted bilayer WSe2 along Γ-K over the full monolayer Brillouin zone.
Zoomed ARPES spectral function near Γ comparing twisted and untwisted WSe2, highlighting the additional split features in the twisted sample.
ARPES band dispersions near Γ along both high-symmetry directions, showing the moiré miniband structure.
Calculated band dispersion and spectral weights from the effective continuum Hamiltonian for V0 = 48 meV and phase ϕ = 121°.
figures.data_type: theory band structure / spectral weight plot

figures.description: Stack of EDCs along K-Γ-K' with Lorentzian peak positions marked from curve fits.
Curvature plot of the ARPES dispersion along K-Γ-K'.
Curvature plot of the ARPES dispersion along M-Γ-M'.
Simulated spectral function from the effective Hamiltonian corresponding to the K-Γ-K' direction.
Simulated spectral function from the effective Hamiltonian corresponding to the M-Γ-M' direction.

paper_analysis.raw_measurement_details: The paper reports raw experimental measurements from micro-ARPES (band dispersions, constant-energy maps, energy distribution curves) and micro-LEED (diffraction patterns used to extract the moiré wavelength and twist angle). It also includes AFM topography of the heterostructure surface/device. The text does not specify file formats, but the measurements are described as synchrotron-based microfocus ARPES at Diamond Light Source and micro-LEED characterization.
paper_analysis.processed_plot_details: Processed plots include Lorentzian/Gaussian fits to EDCs, curvature plots of ARPES dispersions, extracted peak positions, and simulated spectral functions from an effective continuum Hamiltonian. The moiré potential V0 and phase ϕ are obtained by least-squares comparison of >200000 calculated spectral functions to the raw ARPES data.

dataset_characterization.data_availability_statement: The paper states: 'The research data supporting this publication can be accessed at the Yareta repository of the University of Geneva [51].' It also points to Supplemental Material for additional information on sample fabrication, characterization, and experimental methods.
dataset_characterization.raw_data_description: Raw data are implied to include micro-ARPES spectra/maps, micro-LEED diffraction images, and AFM images collected on the tWSe2 heterostructure. The paper does not specify the repository file structure, file formats, or whether all raw detector outputs are deposited.
dataset_characterization.processed_data_description: Processed data include fitted EDC peak positions, extracted valley-energy differences, curvature plots, and quantitative parameters from model fitting (e.g. V0 = 48 ± 8 meV, ϕ = 121° ± 7°, twist angle 57.4 ± 0.1°). The paper also describes simulated spectral functions used for comparison with experiment.
dataset_characterization.figure_data_description: Figure-specific data are clearly present in the sense of plotted experimental and fitted curves, but the paper does not explicitly state that per-figure source-data files are provided. The repository is mentioned, but the exact contents are not described in the text.
dataset_characterization.scripts_description: No analysis scripts, notebooks, or code repositories are mentioned in the paper text. The continuum-model calculations are described conceptually, but no script availability is stated.
dataset_characterization.replot_reason: The paper indicates that research data are available in a repository and provides enough quantitative information to reproduce key plots in principle, but the text does not specify whether the deposited data are raw detector outputs, figure source data, or both. Replotting is likely possible for some figures if the repository contains the plotted datasets, but this cannot be confirmed from the paper text alone.
dataset_characterization.notes: The paper strongly distinguishes between raw experimental measurements and derived/model-based analysis. The most important derived quantities are the twist angle from μLEED, the valley-energy splitting from EDC fitting, and the moiré potential from large-scale spectral-function fitting. The text explicitly mentions >200000 calculated spectral functions, indicating a computational parameter sweep used for model fitting, but not a deposited script or grid file. The repository reference suggests data availability, but the exact dataset semantics remain partially unspecified.

classification_prior.raw_data_evidence: The paper reports direct experimental measurements: micro-ARPES maps/EDCs, micro-LEED diffraction patterns, and AFM topography, all of which are raw measurement modalities.
classification_prior.source_data_evidence: The paper presents fitted EDC peak positions, curvature plots, extracted twist angle and moiré parameters, and simulated spectral functions, which are processed/figure-source data rather than raw detector output.
classification_prior.both_evidence: The paper contains both direct measurements and derived/fit-based datasets used for the figures and parameter extraction.
data_availability_section_relevant: The research data supporting this publication can be accessed at the Yareta repository of the University of Geneva [51].
See Supplemental Material ... for additional information on sample fabrication, characterization, and experimental methods.
To do so in an unbiased way we calculate > 200000 spectral functions for different parameters and determine the least square difference to the raw data.
classification_prior.review_notes: Do not assume the repository contains scripts or explicitly labeled source-data files; the paper only guarantees research data availability. The continuum-model simulations are derived data, not raw measurements. The phrase 'source data' is not used in the paper, so any source-data classification should remain cautious and based on the plotted/fitted datasets rather than explicit author labeling.
